item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image comes from here and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki’s license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed the image because it wasn't wiki compliant. Hope this new one is okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes I know. I probably didn't make it clear enough that the note is supposed to contradict previous information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I probably didn't make myself clear in the second issue. These two sentences:

In a seemingly random interval ranging from 3 to 24 hours, the location of SCP-3423-1 will instantly change.

Note: Neither the interval of change nor the location is random.

Contradict each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what? Those are actually really valid points…

1) I guess I just wanted to make a relatively harmless SCP and I failed to realise it's full potential. However I think that if anything, it should be a Euclid.

2) The decision to keep it as a note was a simple one, I think I prefer it in this case rather than a crossed out sentence but that might be just me.

For the second question, I don't know. That idea never really crossed my mind. Maybe you have to leave something to the mystery.

Thanks for reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two issues I have with this:

1) Why is it Safe? Euclid or even Keter seems more appropriate given the fact SCP-3423-1 can appear anywhere.

2)

In a seemingly random interval ranging from 3 to 24 hours, the location of SCP-3423-1 will instantly change.

Note: Neither the interval of change nor the location is random.

Shouldn't the first sentence be crossed-out, or replaced by the note? Also, does SCP-3423-1 still change location if no-one is near it? Because if that's the case, then that would make the note only partially correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am very supprised that this triggered such a response. It was just me trying to put the idea of threat level into it because I kind of like it, there is nothing extra deep about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it works as an idea, it works; I don't take issue with it. But doing it as a "screw you" to the established format is petty childishness.

I highly doubt that's how the author here intended it to be taken, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's more of an aesthetic and a screw you to the established format imo

Makes articles look that much better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A lot of authors strongly disagree with the idea of threat levels.

They don't add any narrative depth (in my opinion) like an object class does. It's just a straightforward "this is how dangerous it is!" which always leaves me feeling a bit irritable off the bat.

Don't tell me how dangerous it is, show me. Let me realize why this thing is bad news bears for humanity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the threat level concept that, I think is being tried? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edits made in conference with Utylike!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Switching to DMs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I guess it would be the right course of action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a basic grammar/spelling/"naturalization" edit of the SCP done.

Right now, I'm working on a slight modification of it to fit with tone & standard SCP-ness. I can show you the first edit if you like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My pleasure. I'll try to work on it tonight, or tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! I would be happy if somone helped me. Feel free to give suggestions in any way you see fit. Thanks for the willingness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote for the moment. Would you like help with making this sound more naturally English? I'd be happy to go through and edit/give you suggestions. Just on the wording and such, not the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the critique. You know, this is my first SCP and my first language isn't english. Still. I shouldn't make such mistakes. If you have any advice on what I can do to make it better, that would be great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No-vote. I liked the concept, but this needs more editing to fix obvious grammatical and phrasing errors. Additionally, it feels underdeveloped as a whole.

The last addendum felt really contrived. My first thought upon getting the gist of the entry was "oh wow, the Foundation could totally use this for intelligence and scientific purposes." I assume that the last bit was added to address the dearth of that material, but I don't think that it was convincing enough to succeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. Just a small question. What do you mean by "a bit odd". Could you specify? I will take a look at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was just going to be an inspirational poster but a window. +1 for not doing exactly what I expected. Though the quotation marks at the begining of some sentences are a bit odd, don’t know what’s up with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The final Addendum feels a tad extraneous, but otherwise an interesting little entry.

+1

item.title: Thanks, everyone
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who contributed with advice, help and explanation. Unfortunately I am not a person to remember names and it has been a few months since I started this. So my thanks is to everyone. Even those who stop by only to downvote. Your opinions matter to me.

EDIT: Thanks to DrScaramouche for helping with some of the parts and overall for help on perfecting the SCP.
